channel.title: Hugo O. Rivera
channel.description: Recent content on Hugo O. Rivera

item.title: advent-of-code-2019
item.description: https://adventofcode.com/2019

item.title: i3empty
item.description: Quickly switch to a new numbered workspace. Utility for the i3 window manager.

item.title: Intro to programming using Python at Equipo Academy’s College Simulation Day
item.description: A programming tutorial.

item.title: HelloWorlds
item.description: What is programming? Here you'll find lots of introductory projects for many of different applications written in a variety of notable programming languages.

item.title: HelloWorlds
item.description: What is programming? Here you'll find lots of introductory projects for many of different applications written in a variety of notable programming languages.

item.title: tetris-for-two
item.description: Tetris for two. A fun tetris game with many different game modes

item.title: Interview Prep
item.description: Advice I found useful.

item.title: New Science
item.description: Complexity Accept chaos. Machine Learning Applied mathematics with the power of modern supercomputers. Advanced optimization, linear algebra, calculus. Effectiveness of techniques revolutionized by deep learning. Climate Change Consequences of pollution known for about a century. Non-linear effects expected to cause disruptions.

item.title: New Tech
Animated Periodic Table D3
item.description: A periodic table created in CoffeeScript and D3 with animated transitions.

item.title: Quotes, notable snippets
item.description: Fearful Thing to Love by Yehuda HaLevi (Judah HaLevi) Tis a Fearful Thing ‘Tis a fearful thing to love what death can touch. A fearful thing to love, to hope, to dream, to be – to be, And oh, to lose. A thing for fools, this, And a holy thing, a holy thing to love. For your life has lived in me, your laugh once lifted me, your word was gift to me.

item.title: Scientific Evidence of Worsening Effects of the Human Pollution Epidemic
item.description: Google it. In summary: don't look and trust climate scientists. The good news is climate change activism is working. A Useful Framework for Dealing With This: If you're interested in what kind of leadership it might take to guide all the peoples of the world out of this crisis, I invite you to look into Deep Adaptation. This is a professional and scientific term for the cultural and societal changes that will be needed to help the world transition maintain a more sustainable future without resorting to violence, pessimistic nihilism, paranoia of human extinction, or other fallacies similar ideologies may fall into.

item.title: DACA Timeline
item.description: A timelapse of the legal battles that have been tormenting me in the past couple of years.

item.title: Code of Honor
item.description: Silence is golden.

item.title: Programmer-for-hire
item.description: I may be available for software engineering contracting work. With my full stack skillset, I can create a webapp from start to finish. Programming Languages I know the following languages: Python including Tensorflow and other machine learning and data science tools. JavaScript including ES6, NodeJS, and other web development tools C C++, including Qt and the language QML Java Haskell Elm Lisp Rust I have experience with the Linux operating system.

item.title: Tutoring
item.description: I am your math, language, and programming tutor Qualifications: I worked as a tutor at the Computer Science department of the New Mexico Institute of Mining and Technology for two years. I have a degree in mathematics and a degree in computer science from the same school, where I graduated with a 3.5 GPA in 4.5 years. I tutored middle-school as part of the Growing Up Thinking Scientifically (GUTS) program and high-school students in the Supercomputing Challenge.

item.title: Internet Marketing
item.description: Hi, I am your internet marketing guy Read more and contact me via emarketing.roguh.com Haga clic aquí para más información en publicista.roguh.com Skillset I have helped two small business in the USA build a compelling online presence. Contact me to talk about my portfolio and to get a free consultation. I can do the following: Help you find an adequate domain name such as yourbusiness.com. Help you receive email at a professional looking address, such as ceo@yourbusiness.

item.title: DACA Effects
item.description: Benefits Freedom to travel to and live in all 50 states Can work legally in most US companies Can buy stock in the US stock market Obliged to pay the same taxes as any citizen Drawbacks No international flights Can't obtain security clearance Can't work in the military Can't work in the aerospace industry Can't vote at any level of government Difficulty in finding funding for education Must give sensitive personal information to the government Part of the USA's immigration debate.

item.title: Me + DACA
item.description: Check out a timeline of the DACA immigration program I rarely broadcast politics or personal stressors. I worry my years of study have gone to waste due to political stupidity at various levels from numerous groups. I was undocumented until I was able to work in this great nation using the DACA program, which has stood on tenuous ground since late 2017. I was brought to the USA as a child.

item.title: You, Node.js Development, and Docker
item.description: My take on developing Node.js projects using Docker.

item.title: cheatsheets
item.description: Toolkit for converting notes into compact PDFs suitable for quick reference sheets, cheatsheets, cribsheets, etc. Notes can be written in markdown, are rendered to PDF by Pandoc.

item.title: passwordstore-noise-generator
item.description: Patches up a possible side-channel in zx2c4's pass password manager.

item.title: nodejs-api-starter
item.description: Boilerplate and tooling for authoring data API backends with Node.js and GraphQL. A personal fork.

item.title: Linux and MacOS configuration files
item.description: My portable, personal, developer-centric setup for common Linux and MacOS tools.

item.title: Some General Cybersecurity Advice
item.description: A mishmash of what could be common-sense security tips and other ideas for preventing security leaks on personal devices.

item.title: Security News
item.description: Two of my cybersecurity courses at NMT asked students to write reports on recent security news. This continues to be a hobby of mine. Fascinating exploits: And of course, I have some preventative measures for various types of cyber attacks and cyber nuisances.

item.title: git-quick-stats
item.description: A personal fork of ▁▅▆▃▅ git-quick-statistics, a simple and efficient way to access various statistics in git repository

item.title: ocaml-lox
item.description: Lox interpreter written in OCaml.

item.title: scss-colors
item.description: Find all variables with color values in SCSS files.

item.title: Contact Information
item.description: hugo@roguh.com +1 (774) 354-9367 linkedin.com/in/hugoorivera/ Download my contact info (VCard .VCF format) keybase.io/roguh for encrypted communications. I'm also available on Signal and Telegram. Occasionally available on Facebook at facebook.com/hugoroguh. I do not recommend using any of Zuckerberg's software..

item.title: Aliases + Names
item.description: My full name is Hugo Omar Rivera Calzadillas. I prefer the Spanish pronunciation oo-GOH. I use Hugo O. Rivera for the sake of conciseness, memorability, and SEO. Many institutions are unaware of or unwilling to accommodate the rich diversity of personal names around the world; therefore, I use: Hugo Rivera Hugo Omar Rivera Hugo O. Rivera-Calzadillas Hugo Rivera-Calzadillas Hugo O. Rivera Calzadillas Hugo Rivera Calzadillas Screen names roguh (ro-KOO): Inspired by Rokurōta Makabe, a character in one of my favorite movies, The Hidden Fortress directed by Akira Kurosawa.

item.title: pympw
item.description: A Python implementation of the Master Password algorithm v3 with a nice Linux CLI

item.title: Best Websites
item.description: In order, as determined by the knowledge graph and me: https://github.com/resir014/thebestmotherfuckingwebsite.co https://motherfuckingwebsite.com/ https://thebestmotherfucking.website/ http://bettermotherfuckingwebsite.com/ https://evenbettermotherfucking.website/ https://perfectmotherfuckingwebsite.com/ https://thebestmotherfuckingwebsite.co/

item.title: polybar-themer
item.description: A theme generator for polybar, a popular status bar for Linux. Uses math and advanced YAML parsing to generate colorful, sensible color schemes.

item.title: ocaml-parsing
item.description: Boilerplate code for writing parsers in OCaml using Menhir + sedlex

item.title: cuda-fft
item.description: Yet another FFT implementation in CUDA. Includes benchmarks using simple data for comparing different implementations.

item.title: pandoc-resume
item.description: Write a résumé in markdown and convert to PDF, DOCX, and HTML.

item.title: ansible-fwknop
item.description: Ansible role that secures ports on your server from unauthorized access using fwknop, an improved port knocking utility.

item.title: GameMaker mirrors
item.description: GameMaker game decompilers

item.title: POC||GTFO mirrors
item.description: Some reliable POC||GTFO mirrors

item.title: PhotoFest
item.description: PhotoFest is a secure self-hosted flat-file photo collection webapp written in Rust (unfinished)

item.title: email-classifier
item.description: A machine learning project for classifying emails into spam and ham. Uses C for preprocessing emails, Python for parsing and extracting low-level features of emails, and a few lines of Matlab for running several classic classifiers.

item.title: qtdemo
item.description: A test of the features of the C++ framework Qt5, especially for Android apps.

item.title: coolnoise
item.description: Parser and generator for programs written in the Classroom Object-Oriented Language. Eases testing of lexer and parser.

item.title: New Mexico Tech's Introduction to Python and Programming Labs (CS 107)
item.description: Carefully written and formatted. I wrote much of the copy-editing and some of the LaTeX for this year's version of the labs.

item.title: cv-composer
item.description: Command-line based computer vision algorithms showcase. Uses the OpenCV C++ library.

item.title: hopfield_tests
item.description: These Python scripts can be used to test the recall effectiveness of Hopfield Networks. A sample dataset composed of the Walsh vectors, an orthogonal system is included by default.

item.title: Notes on Chemistry the Central Science
item.description: See github.com/roguh/chemistry_the_central_science.

item.title: Notes on Perception for Design
item.description: I took concise notes based on the excellent, scientifically-rigorous textbook Information Visualization: Perception for Design by Colin Ware. I created this PDF using LaTeX, which allowed me the flexibility to create an easy-to-edit, elegant, and compact document layout. I highly recommend the book and these notes for anyone that needs to create data visualizations. See here.

item.title: CORE
item.description: Implementation of SPJ's Tutorial Core compilers in modern Haskell (unfinished)

item.title: SCMinHS
item.description: A Scheme Implementation in Haskell. Followed “Write Yourself a Scheme in 48 Hours.”

item.title: Darkrose and Diamond
item.description: Darkrose and Diamond A Boat Song from West Havnor Where my love is going There will I go. Where his boat is rowing I will row. We will laugh together, Together we will cry. If he lives I will live, If he dies I die. Where my love is going There will I go. Where his boat is rowing I will row. IN THE WEST OF HAVNOR, among hills forested with oak and chestnut, is the town of Glade.

item.title: A brief autobiography
item.description: Education and career I studied computer science and mathematics at the Santa Fe Community College in NM. During this time, I taught myself enough Haskell to write most of a simple compiler for Haskell's intermediate language Core. I'd tutor fellow students in my Python course. I volunteered as a generalist technician, repairman, and software person at Computer Charity, Inc. in Santa Fe. My grades meant I qualified for the Phi Theta Kappa honor society's scholarship which allowed me to attend a four-year university.

item.title: Business Card
item.description: Hugo O. Rivera roguh.com

item.title: Career
item.description: I'm passionate about developing tomorrow's essential technology. I've worked as a full stack software engineer in the San Francisco Bay Area. Fullstack software engineer Bay Area (2018 to 2020) I build software to facilitate adaptive load management for large-scale electric-vehicle charging stations at PowerFlex, a recently acquired subsidiary of EDF Renewables, which is itself the North American branch of the renewable energy branch of an enormous multinational French energy company Électricité de France that powers much of the world using nuclear, solar, and wind energy.

item.title: Hugo O. Rivera's language cheatsheet
item.description: Read about them here

item.title: Hugo O. Rivera's language cheatsheet
item.description: Read about them here

item.title: New Mexico
item.description: The 47th state in the UNITED STATES OF AMERICA. Known for lowriders, U+1F47Ds, green chile, [beautiful wilderness](see 35.938,-106.204), and Meow Wolf's House of Eternal Return.

item.title: USHANKA
item.description: the angles cut me when i try to think it feels like thinking through cheese wire there's no feeling but the no-feeling hurts it's cold and it hurts the maze is sharp on my mind. the angles cut me when i try to think.
